summary: DHH writes a brief, personal update from Chicago about the Cubs' unlikely playoff run, jet lag from traveling from Denmark, and his excitement about receiving a new 17-inch PowerBook. The post is a casual snapshot of his life in late 2003, mixing sports observations with tech enthusiasm. He reflects on a productive but tiring week, framing his experiences in deliberate parallel contrasts. The post is notable mainly as an early example of DHH's personal blogging style before Rails fame.
key_points: The Cubs reaching the playoffs for the first time since 1908 serves as a metaphor for unlikely change
DHH was splitting time between Denmark and Chicago, dealing with persistent jet lag
He frames his week in deliberate contrasts: cold but sunny, work but inspiring, waiting but fulfilling
His excitement about a new 17-inch PowerBook with 1GB RAM reflects the Apple enthusiasm common among early Rails-era developers
money_quotes: The Chicago Cubs haven't swung their way this far into the play-offs since 1908. And I've never cared much about baseball. I guess some things can change.
Cold, but sunny and clear. Work, but inspiring and nearing completion. Wait, but fulfilling at delivery.
key_insight: A casual personal snapshot from DHH's pre-Rails life, notable mainly for its glimpse into the Denmark-Chicago lifestyle and Apple enthusiasm that would later become central to his public identity.
